# npix_ellip_center { #astroapers.npix_ellip_center }

```python
npix_ellip_center(x, y, a, b, theta=0.0, *, shape)
```

Return center-selected elliptical-aperture effective in-frame pixel counts.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**x, y**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [scalar or array_like]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Aperture center coordinates in pixel units. Shapes must match after ``numpy.atleast_1d``. The return shape matches that broadcast-free input shape, so scalar inputs return one-element arrays.

<code>[**a, b**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [float]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Semimajor and semiminor axes in pixels.

<code>[**theta**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [float, optional]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [0.0]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Rotation angle in radians, measured counterclockwise from the positive x axis.

<code>[**shape**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [tuple[int, int]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Image shape as ``(ny, nx)`` used to clip the aperture footprint.

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[**npix**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [ndarray]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Effective pixel count inside the image frame.

## Notes {.doc-section .doc-section-notes}

``center`` mode uses binary weights selected by pixel-center inclusion. The boundary convention matches Photutils for supported shapes: pixels exactly on the outer boundary are excluded, and annuli include the inner boundary while excluding the outer boundary.
